must_not_assert: that active adversarial reconnaissance or malicious intent is confirmed (the package evidences confirmed local-account enumeration but no intent-bearing follow-on such as credential access or lateral movement)
that a compromise or breach resulted (no post-discovery impact is present in this package)

the_trap: Local-account enumeration is confirmed: two distinct discovery techniques — Get-LocalUser (A0) and Get-WmiObject Win32_UserAccount (EV-003) — each launched via a cmd.exe -> powershell.exe chain (EV-001, EV-002), all as corp\user-01 at Medium integrity within a ~9-second window. This is a genuine T1087.001 discovery indicator, but the package evidences the enumeration only; it shows no elevation (integrity_level Medium), no credential access, no lateral movement, and no follow-on. The model must not escalate confirmed discovery into confirmed adversarial recon or compromise. Distinct from DISC-001 (AD/domain) and DISC-002 (system/OS discovery).
rationale: The package confirms local-account discovery — Get-LocalUser (A0) and Get-WmiObject Win32_UserAccount (EV-003), with their cmd.exe launchers EV-001 and EV-002 — all executed by corp\user-01 at Medium integrity across a ~9-second window. What is not evidenced is intent or compromise: the integrity_level is Medium (no privilege elevation), and the package contains no credential access, lateral movement, or persistence, with deterministic_derivations empty and no counter-evidence. Account discovery is confirmed; adversarial intent or compromise is not. This is a real but low-signal discovery indicator with ambiguous intent: suspicious and worth investigating, not confirmed recon.
